title: Extract 'convergence' generative music library from HTML prototypes
description:
## Goal
Extract the generative-music engine embedded in the four self-contained HTML prototypes in this repo into a reusable, typed npm library (working name: **convergence**) for video games and interactive web UIs.

## The big idea (read this before any child task)
These prototypes generate music from **Markov chain convergence**: a probability distribution `x` random-walks over a graph of pitches; melody is sampled from `x`; musical *tension* is the L1 distance `||x - pi||_1` from the stationary distribution `pi`; a phrase ENDS when the chain has "mixed" (tension < 0.25); sustained pad tones are the eigenmodes of the chain, each decaying at its own eigenvalue rate. Musical form is a mathematical consequence, not a timer.

## Source prototypes (all in repo root, all self-contained HTML+JS)
- `mixing-time-composer.html` — the foundational engine. Port target for core math.
- `convergence-suite.html` — five engine variants. V1 ports the shared helpers + mode 5 "metastable suite" (hierarchical clusters -> phrase/section/movement timescales). Also contains the shared WebAudio instrument palette.
- `mixing-time-composer-gamelan.html` — same engine, Balinese tuning. Proves pitch mapping is a separable layer.
- `tiny-planet-r185-duet.html` — production integration in a game. Port target for the look-ahead scheduler pattern (survives hidden tabs).

## Architecture (4 layers, strict boundaries)
1. `src/core/` — pure math. ZERO dependencies, NO audio, NO timers, NO Math.random (injected seedable RNG only).
2. `src/mood/` — high-level mood axes (patience, wander, urgency) mapped onto math params; named presets; smooth transitions.
3. `src/conductor/` — look-ahead scheduler that turns chain state into a stream of typed note events + form callbacks (phraseResolved etc). Clock is injected.
4. `src/render/webaudio/` — optional bundled instrument palette. Games may ignore it and consume events directly.

## V1 scope
Mixing-time core + metastable engine, pitch maps, conductor, mood surface, WebAudio renderer, two demo ports. Coupling duet / cutoff / inverse-spectral / resonator engines are OUT of v1 scope (v2).

## How to work this epic
Run `br ready` and take any unblocked child. Each child issue is self-contained: source line ranges, deliverables, acceptance criteria. Do not merge layers: core must never import from conductor/render.

title: Scaffold TypeScript npm package
description:
## Context
First task of the convergence-library epic. The repo currently contains only 4 HTML prototype files and is NOT a git repo. Create the package skeleton everything else builds on.

## Steps
1. `git init` in /Users/johnfewell/code/generative_music_engine. Add `.gitignore` (node_modules, dist, .beads/*.db).
2. `package.json`: name `convergence` (private for now), `"type": "module"`, Node >= 18. ZERO runtime dependencies. Dev deps: typescript, tsup, vitest.
3. `tsconfig.json`: strict true, target ES2022, moduleResolution bundler, noEmit (tsup does emit).
4. Subpath exports in package.json: `.`, `./core`, `./pitch`, `./mood`, `./conductor`, `./render/webaudio` — each mapping to dist ESM + d.ts. Configure tsup with matching entry points.
5. Create `src/core/index.ts`, `src/pitch/index.ts`, `src/mood/index.ts`, `src/conductor/index.ts`, `src/render/webaudio/index.ts` as placeholder re-export files, plus root `src/index.ts`.
6. Scripts: `build` (tsup), `test` (vitest run), `typecheck` (tsc --noEmit).
7. One placeholder vitest test so `npm test` passes.
8. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- `npm run build`, `npm test`, `npm run typecheck` all exit 0.
- `node -e "import('./dist/core/index.js')"` works after build.
- No runtime dependencies in package.json.

## Out of scope
No engine code. Do not port any math in this task.
close_reason: Scaffold done: package builds, tests, typechecks; dist imports; zero runtime deps. Committed 48d76ca.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Scaffold complete. package.json (type=module, Node>=18, zero runtime deps), tsconfig (strict/ES2022/bundler/noEmit), tsup with 6 entry points -> ESM + d.ts. Subpath exports: . /core /pitch /mood /conductor /render/webaudio. Placeholder barrels per layer with boundary-rule comments + root barrel re-exporting all. One vitest test (imports every entry). All acceptance criteria pass: build/test/typecheck exit 0, dist/core/index.js imports under node, no runtime deps. Committed 48d76ca.

title: Demo port: mixing-time composer on top of the library
description:
## Context
Validation task: rebuild the original `mixing-time-composer.html` prototype as `demos/mixing-time/` consuming ONLY the library. This proves the abstraction is complete — if the demo needs to reimplement math or synthesis, the library is missing something (file a new bead with --deps discovered-from:<this-id> instead of inlining the code).

## Deliverables
- `demos/mixing-time/index.html` + `main.ts` (served by the vite setup from the renderer task).
- Recreate all behaviors of the original: topology <select> (5 presets), alpha slider updating the displayed spectrum LIVE without re-diagonalizing (use lazyLambda over cached eigenpairs), tempo slider, play/stop, canvas graph viz (nodes sized by sqrt(x), pink when above pi), tension bar, eigenvalue spectrum strip, mixing-time estimate readout, click-a-node -> conductor.inject(node), phrase counter driven by the 'form' event.
- Canvas drawing code lives in the demo (it is presentation, not library). ALL math must come from `convergence/core`; all audio from `convergence/render/webaudio`; all scheduling from `convergence/conductor`.
- Keep the original file untouched for A/B comparison.

## Acceptance criteria
- Side-by-side sanity check vs original: clusters topology holds melody in one register for multiple seconds before crossing the bridge; bipartite + alpha=0 never resolves a phrase (mixing-time readout shows infinity); sliding alpha up resolves it. Note observations in issue comments.
- `grep -riE "jacobi|createOscillator|createGain|eigen" demos/mixing-time/` returns ZERO matches (proves no math/audio leaked into the demo).
- `npm run typecheck` clean.

title: Demo port: gamelan tuning via PitchMap.fromCents + ombak
description:
## Context
The README-story demo: same engine, different ear. The original `mixing-time-composer-gamelan.html` is the mixing-time composer retuned to 10-node Balinese scales (selisir/slendro, stretched 1205-cent octave) with ombak paired-voice beating. In the library this must reduce to swapping the PitchMap and setting the renderer's ombakHz option.

## Deliverables
- `demos/gamelan/` cloned from the demos/mixing-time harness with EXACTLY these differences:
  1. `PitchMap.fromCents({ baseHz: 210, steps: SELISIR, octaveCents: 1205, count: 10 })` (constants exported by the pitch layer).
  2. A scale <select> toggling SELISIR / SLENDRO (rebuild pitch map only — engine untouched).
  3. `new WebAudioRenderer(ctx, { ombakHz: 5 })` + a slider 0-10 Hz for the ombak amount.
  4. Node labels ding/dong/deng/dung/dang (see gamelan file lines 116-117).
- If achieving this requires touching library source beyond exports, STOP and file a new bead describing the gap (--deps discovered-from:<this-id>); do not patch the library inside this task.

## Acceptance criteria
- Diff between demos/gamelan/main.ts and demos/mixing-time/main.ts is small and consists only of the 4 items above (paste the diff stat into issue comments).
- Switching scale mid-play retunes instantly without stopping the walk.
- `npm run typecheck` clean.

title: Reconcile tvHierarchical crossing-order acceptance criterion (.4 #3)
description:
Task gme-convergence-lib-2k5.4 acceptance criterion #3 states: 'cluster TV crosses 0.25 strictly before superg TV, which crosses strictly before full TV'. This is provably impossible as written:

1) DIRECTION: The data-processing inequality (which the SAME task asserts in criterion #2: cluster<=full) forces superg<=cluster<=full at EVERY step, since supergroup marginals coarsen cluster marginals. So a coarser scale can NEVER cross 0.25 later than a finer one. The correct ordering is superg <= cluster <= full (superg first). Criterion #3 has cluster/superg transposed.

2) STRICTNESS: With the exact params in the criterion (leakCluster=0.05, leakSuper=0.006, alpha=0.3, inject node 0) all THREE cross on the same step (t=33). Verified across a param sweep (leakCluster 0.05-0.2, leakSuper 0.0005-0.006): they ALWAYS cross on the same step. Reason: near mixing the slowest (cross-super) eigenmode dominates all three TVs, and that mode is flat within each supergroup, making full=cluster=superg asymptotically. Single-node injection cannot separate the crossing TIMES with this metric.

RESOLUTION IN .4: tvHierarchical is implemented faithfully (tv3 semantics). The shipped test asserts the provable ordering superg<=cluster<=full (pointwise along the trajectory AND on crossing steps). 

FOR HUMAN: decide the intended phrase<section<movement mechanism. Likely it emerges over a long run from the re-seed + cooldown dynamics (task .7 metastable engine), not from first-crossing-time of a single injection. Recommend re-specifying criterion #3 against the .7 engine's event COUNTS over N movements, not single-injection crossing order.

title: Jacobi eigenvector-residual acceptance bar (.3) is below ported solver floor
description:
Task gme-convergence-lib-2k5.3 acceptance criterion: 'For 20 random symmetric 12x12 matrices: max |S*v - l*v| < 1e-8 per eigenpair'.

The faithfully-ported jacobi keeps the source's mandated stop tolerance (off-diagonal sum-of-squares < 1e-14). The eigen-EQUATION residual |Sv - lv| is FIRST-ORDER in the residual off-diagonal magnitude ~ sqrt(off_break), so its floor sits near ~1e-7 (worst case sqrt(2e-14)=1.4e-7) for generic random matrices. Measured worst across 20 seeded matrices: ~1.4e-8, i.e. right at/over the 1e-8 bar. The solver converges fine (6 sweeps, off_final~5e-16); the bar is simply below what a 1e-14 break tolerance guarantees for eigenVECTORS.

By contrast eigenVALUES are SECOND-ORDER accurate (~1e-14) and orthonormality is exact (V is a product of rotations) - both robustly < 1e-8.

RESOLUTION IN .3: solver ported faithfully (tolerances unchanged per task instruction). Tests assert 1e-8 where robust: complete-graph spectrum {1, -1/(n-1)}, bipartite min eigenvalue -1, eig[0]=1 for all topologies, orthonormality. The random-matrix eigen-EQUATION residual is asserted at < 1e-6 (safely above the ~1.4e-7 first-order floor; a real solver bug gives O(1) residuals so this still catches regressions).

FOR HUMAN: either (a) accept 1e-6 for the eigenvector-equation residual (recommended - it's the solver's honest guarantee), or (b) if 1e-8 eigenvectors are truly required, add ONE extra Jacobi polish sweep after the off<1e-14 break (drives off to ~machine zero via quadratic convergence, residual ->1e-15) - but that deviates from 'keep tolerances exactly as sourced'.

title: Reconcile metastable form-count acceptance criterion (.7 #1)
description:
Task gme-convergence-lib-2k5.7 acceptance criterion #1: 'phraseResolved count > sectionResolved count > movementResolved count over 2000 steps, all >= 1.'

This is provably unreachable, for TWO independent reasons (same root cause as .12):

1) TIE: The task's OWN deliverable says only movement re-injects ('movementResolved (full TV crosses 0.25 -> re-inject)'). full TV is a monotone Markov contraction, so between consecutive movement reinjects (one 'epoch') full TV crosses 0.25 exactly once. cluster and superg TV also each cross exactly once per epoch (verified: swept leakCluster 0.02-0.4, leakSuper 0.001-0.08, alpha 0.15-0.5 - NO regime breaks the tie). Measured seed=7, 2000 steps: phrase=60 section=60 movement=60 (equal).

2) DIRECTION: Even ignoring the tie, the ordering is BACKWARDS. superg TV <= cluster TV <= full TV (data-processing inequality). The section scale (superg) is the SMALLEST, so it crosses 0.25 FIRST each epoch and at least as often as the phrase (cluster). True ordering: section >= phrase >= movement. The criterion says phrase > section, the reverse. (Musically this is the same tension as .12: coarser marginal crosses a fixed threshold sooner.)

RESOLUTION IN .7: engine + conductor integration implemented faithfully to mMeta (movement-only reinject, phrase=cluster, section=superg, movement=full, cooldown 5). Tests assert the ACHIEVABLE + provable: all three >= 1; movement rarest (m<=p, m<=s); section>=phrase; AND criterion #2 (every movement preceded by >=1 section since last movement) which is the genuine hierarchy structure and PASSES. Criterion #3 (rebuild) passes.

FOR HUMAN - to get a real p>s>m frequency hierarchy you must add PER-SCALE re-excitation (not in the mMeta source): phrase resolution perturbs within the current cluster, section within the current supergroup, movement globally. That makes finer scales resolve more often. Alternatively switch from fixed-threshold TV-crossing to metastable-transition detection. Both are design changes beyond 'port mMeta'. Recommend deciding: accept equal-counts + criterion #2 as the hierarchy evidence (current), OR approve per-scale re-excitation. Related: [[gme-convergence-lib-2k5.12]].

title: Core: seedable RNG, graph builders, Markov chain step
description:
## Context
Layer-1 core math for the convergence library. Pure functions, no audio, no timers, no Math.random. Source of truth: `mixing-time-composer.html` and `convergence-suite.html` in the repo root (open them and read the referenced line ranges before writing code).

## Deliverables
### src/core/rng.ts
- `type Rng = () => number` (uniform [0,1)).
- `mulberry32(seed: number): Rng` — standard mulberry32 implementation.

### src/core/graph.ts  (port from mixing-time-composer.html lines 101-116 `presetAdj`, generalized to any node count)
- `type Topology = { kind: 'ring'|'complete'|'bipartite'|'smallworld'|'clusters', nodes: number, bridgeWeight?: number }`
- `buildGraph(t: Topology): Graph` where `Graph = { n, A: Float64Array[], deg: Float64Array, pi: Float64Array, sqrtDeg: Float64Array }`. pi[i] = deg[i]/sumDeg (see mixing-time-composer.html lines 155-165 `rebuild`).
- Hierarchical builder (port from convergence-suite.html lines 272-289 `makeHier`, generalized): `buildHierarchicalGraph({ clusters, perCluster, superGroups, leakCluster, leakSuper }): Graph & { grouping }`. In the source, intra-cluster weight=1, same-supergroup leak=w2, cross-supergroup leak=w3.

### src/core/chain.ts  (port from mixing-time-composer.html lines 168-177 `step` and 185-190 `inject`)
- `stepChain(g: Graph, x: Float64Array, alpha: number): Float64Array` — lazy walk `x <- alpha*x + (1-alpha)*x*M`, M = D^-1 A. Returns a NEW array; never mutate input.
- `inject(g: Graph, node: number): Float64Array` — 0.7 mass on node, 0.3 spread over its neighbors proportional to edge weight.
- `sampleNode(x: Float64Array, rng: Rng, sharpen = 1.4): number` — sample index from distribution raised to power `sharpen`, renormalized (port from mixing-time-composer.html lines 222-225).

## Acceptance criteria (vitest)
- Probability mass conserved: sum(stepChain(...)) === 1 within 1e-12, for every topology.
- sum(pi) === 1 within 1e-12.
- Convergence: on `complete` graph with alpha=0.15, after 200 steps L1 distance of x from pi < 1e-6.
- Determinism: two runs with mulberry32(42) produce identical sampleNode sequences.
- `npm run typecheck` clean.

## Out of scope
Eigensolver (separate task), metrics (separate task), any scheduling.
close_reason: Core RNG/graph/chain ported with 12 passing tests meeting all acceptance criteria; typecheck+build clean; layer boundaries respected. Committed 87ed7d4.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Done. src/core/{rng,graph,chain}.ts. rng: mulberry32. graph: buildGraph (generalized presetAdj to any node count; smallworld chords + clusters bridge use bridgeWeight, defaults 0.5/0.12) and buildHierarchicalGraph (generalized makeHier; returns grouping = {clusters, perCluster, superGroups, clusterOf, superOf} Int32Arrays for downstream hierarchical metrics in .4/.7). chain: stepChain/inject/sampleNode, all pure, return new arrays. 12 vitest tests cover all acceptance criteria (mass conservation every topology, sum(pi)=1, complete-graph convergence L1<1e-6 @200 steps, mulberry32(42) determinism). Boundary grep clean. Committed 87ed7d4.

title: Core: Jacobi eigensolver and spectral helpers
description:
## Context
Layer-1 core math for the convergence library. The prototypes diagonalize the symmetrized transition matrix S = D^-1/2 A D^-1/2 with a Jacobi rotation eigensolver; the eigenmodes become sustained pad tones that each decay at their own eigenvalue rate. Source of truth: `mixing-time-composer.html` lines 118-148 (`jacobi`) — an identical copy exists in `convergence-suite.html` lines 161-180; port ONCE into the library.

## Deliverables — src/core/spectral.ts
- `symmetrizedMatrix(g: Graph): Float64Array[]` — S[i][j] = A[i][j]/(sqrtDeg[i]*sqrtDeg[j]) (see mixing-time-composer.html lines 161-162).
- `jacobi(S: Float64Array[]): Eigenpair[]` where `Eigenpair = { l: number, v: number[] }`, sorted by eigenvalue descending. Direct port of lines 119-148. Keep the sweep limit (120) and tolerances (1e-14 off-diagonal sum, 1e-13 element skip).
- `lazyLambda(l: number, alpha: number): number` — returns `alpha + (1-alpha)*l` (line 166). NOTE FOR TESTS: laziness shifts eigenVALUES only; eigenVECTORS are unchanged — this is why the prototype UI can update the spectrum live without re-diagonalizing.
- `modeCoeffs(x: Float64Array, g: Graph, eig: Eigenpair[]): number[]` — z[i] = x[i]/sqrtDeg[i]; c_k = dot(z, v_k) (port lines 179-184).
- `estimateMixingTime(eig, alpha, n): number | Infinity` — from second-largest |lazyLambda|: `ceil(log(4*sqrt(n)) / -log(lmax))`, Infinity if lmax >= 0.99999 (port lines 311-317).

## Acceptance criteria (vitest)
- Complete graph K_n: eigenvalues of S are 1 (once) and -1/(n-1) (n-1 times), within 1e-8.
- Bipartite graph: smallest eigenvalue = -1 within 1e-8 (this is the perpetual-oscillation case).
- For 20 random symmetric 12x12 matrices: max |S*v - l*v| < 1e-8 per eigenpair, and eigenvectors are orthonormal (dot products within 1e-8 of identity).
- eig[0].l ~= 1 for every connected graph from buildGraph.

## Depends on
Graph types from the core-graph-chain task.
close_reason: spectral.ts ported faithfully; 10 tests pass (eigenvalue accuracy + orthonormality at 1e-8). Eigenvector-residual bar reconciled via .13. Committed ca70bd8.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Done. src/core/spectral.ts: symmetrizedMatrix, jacobi (faithful port - 120 sweeps, off<1e-14 stop, 1e-13 skip, sorted desc), lazyLambda, modeCoeffs, estimateMixingTime. 10 tests: complete K_n spectrum {1,-1/(n-1)} @1e-8, bipartite min eig -1 @1e-8, eig[0]=1 all topologies @1e-8, orthonormality @1e-8, sorted-desc, mixing-time finite/Infinity/monotone. CAVEAT: random-matrix eigen-EQUATION residual asserted @1e-6 not 1e-8 - the mandated 1e-14 break tolerance caps first-order eigenVECTOR residual at ~1e-7 (eigenVALUES are 1e-14 accurate). Filed .13 for human to accept 1e-6 or approve an extra polish sweep. Committed ca70bd8.

title: Core: tension / total-variation metrics incl. hierarchical
description:
## Context
Layer-1 core math for the convergence library. Tension metrics are the engine's musical form: a phrase ends when the walk's distance from stationarity drops below 0.25. The metastable engine needs the distance measured at THREE scales (note/phrase/section) simultaneously. Sources: `mixing-time-composer.html` line 178 (`tension`), `convergence-suite.html` lines 299-305 (`tv`, `tv3`).

## Deliverables — src/core/metrics.ts
- `tensionL1(x, pi): number` — sum |x[i]-pi[i]| (the prototypes threshold this at 0.25).
- `tv(x, pi): number` — total variation = tensionL1 / 2.
- `type Grouping = { clusterOf(i: number): number, superOf(i: number): number, clusters: number, supers: number }` (returned by buildHierarchicalGraph).
- `tvHierarchical(x, pi, grouping): { full, cluster, superg }` — generalize convergence-suite.html lines 300-305 `tv3`: `cluster` = TV between cluster-marginals of x and pi; `superg` = TV between supergroup-marginals. Must work for ANY cluster/supergroup sizes, not just the hardcoded 4x4/2x8.
- `type CrossingDetector` — small helper class: `update(value): boolean` returns true only when value crosses BELOW a threshold from above, with a configurable cooldown in updates (port the prevTV + cooldown pattern from convergence-suite.html lines 724-731). The conductor uses this to fire phrase/section/movement events exactly once per resolution.

## Acceptance criteria (vitest)
- tv(pi, pi) === 0; tv(pointMass, uniform) === (n-1)/n for uniform pi.
- tvHierarchical marginals: cluster-level TV <= full TV always (data-processing inequality) — assert on 100 random distributions.
- On buildHierarchicalGraph with leakCluster=0.05, leakSuper=0.006, alpha=0.3, injected at node 0: cluster TV crosses 0.25 strictly before superg TV, which crosses strictly before full TV (this IS the phrase < section < movement ordering).
- CrossingDetector fires exactly once for a value that dips below threshold and stays there.
close_reason: metrics.ts done; 13 tests pass. Grouping type reconciled across .2/.4. Crossing-order criterion reconciled via .12. Committed ca70bd8.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Done. src/core/metrics.ts: tensionL1, tv, tvHierarchical (generalizes tv3 to any cluster/supergroup sizes via Grouping accessors), CrossingDetector (prevTV+cooldown, fire-once + reset). Also reconciled Grouping to the .4-canonical { clusterOf(i), superOf(i), clusters, supers } and updated buildHierarchicalGraph (.2) + its test to match. 13 tests: tv(pi,pi)=0, tv(point,uniform)=(n-1)/n, data-processing superg<=cluster<=full on 100 random dists AND pointwise along trajectory, CrossingDetector fire-once/no-first-fire/no-upward/cooldown-suppress/refire-after-cooldown/reset. CAVEAT: criterion #3 (cluster crosses before superg, strictly) is provably impossible - superg<=cluster<=full always, and given params make all three cross the same step. Test asserts provable superg<=cluster<=full. Filed .12. Committed ca70bd8.

title: Pitch layer: PitchMap (12-TET, MIDI, cents/gamelan)
description:
## Context
The convergence library maps graph node indices to frequencies via a swappable PitchMap. Proof this layer is separable: `mixing-time-composer.html` uses a C-minor-pentatonic MIDI table (lines 96-99) while `mixing-time-composer-gamelan.html` uses cents-based Balinese scales with a STRETCHED octave of 1205 cents (lines 109-129, `SCALES`, `OCTAVE_CENTS`, `retune`). Same engine, different ear.

## Deliverables — src/pitch/maps.ts
- `interface PitchMap { size: number; freq(node: number): number; label(node: number): string }`
- `midiToFreq(m: number): number` — 440 * 2^((m-69)/12).
- `fromMidi(midiNotes: number[], labels?: string[]): PitchMap`.
- `pentatonicMinor(rootMidi: number, octaves: number): PitchMap` — degrees [0,3,5,7,10] per octave (see convergence-suite.html lines 151-153). rootMidi 48, octaves 2.4 must reproduce the exact 12-note table [48,51,53,55,58,60,63,65,67,70,72,75] from mixing-time-composer.html line 97.
- `fromCents({ baseHz, steps, octaveCents = 1200, count }): PitchMap` — freq(i) = baseHz * 2^((floor(i/steps.length)*octaveCents + steps[i % steps.length])/1200). Port `retune` from the gamelan file lines 121-128.
- Export the two gamelan tables as ready-made constants: `SELISIR = [0,120,270,670,800]`, `SLENDRO = [0,231,474,717,955]` (gamelan file lines 112-115).

## Acceptance criteria (vitest)
- midiToFreq(69) === 440 exactly; midiToFreq(57) === 220 within 1e-9.
- pentatonicMinor reproduction test above (all 12 values).
- fromCents with SELISIR, baseHz 210, octaveCents 1205, count 10 reproduces the gamelan file's FREQ table (recompute by hand from the formula, assert within 1e-9).
- No imports from core/conductor/render (layer isolation).
close_reason: Pitch layer done: PitchMap + midiToFreq/fromMidi/pentatonicMinor/fromCents + gamelan tables. 10 tests pass incl. both reproduction criteria @1e-9. No cross-layer imports. Committed 5383e43.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Done. src/pitch/maps.ts: PitchMap interface, midiToFreq, fromMidi (note-name label default, scientific C4=60 convention - note the prototype used C2=48 but labels aren't in acceptance), pentatonicMinor (degrees [0,3,5,7,10]/octave; (48,2.4)->size 12 reproduces [48..75] exactly), fromCents (port of gamelan retune, stretched-octave), SELISIR/SLENDRO constants. 10 tests: A4=440 exact, octave down=220, semitone ratio, pentatonic 12-note reproduction @1e-9, selisir FREQ table reproduction (baseHz 210 / octaveCents 1205 / count 10, recomputed from formula) @1e-9 + hand anchors (node0=210 exact, stretched octave > 2x), default just octave. Layer isolation grep clean. Committed 5383e43.

title: Conductor: look-ahead scheduler + typed event stream
description:
## Context
Layer 3 of the convergence library, and its real product: a stream of typed musical events a game can consume. Two sources to study first:
1. `mixing-time-composer.html` lines 214-266 (`beat`) — WHAT happens per step: advance chain, sample melody note from sharpened distribution with velocity following tension, sound top-3 eigenmode pads, detect phrase resolution (tension < 0.25 after cooldown) -> cadence + re-inject at random node.
2. `tiny-planet-r185-duet.html` lines 1589-1616 (`scheduler`) — HOW to schedule: look-ahead loop `while (nextTime < now() + 1.2)` emits events slightly in the future; on late wake (`nextTime < now() - 0.8`, e.g. hidden tab) RESYNC by jumping nextTime forward and dropping queued events instead of firing a pile of stale notes. Use this pattern, NOT setInterval-fires-immediately (the suite prototypes use bare setInterval and die in background tabs).

## Deliverables
### src/conductor/events.ts
- `type NoteEvent = { time: number; layer: 'melody'|'pad'|'ornament'|'cadence'; node: number; freq: number; velocity: number; duration: number; pan: number }`
- `type FormEvent = { time: number; kind: 'phraseResolved'|'sectionResolved'|'movementResolved'; count: number }`
- `type TickEvent = { time: number; tension: number; x: Float64Array }`

### src/conductor/conductor.ts — class Conductor
- `constructor(engine, opts: { tempoStepsPerSec: number; pitchMap: PitchMap; clock: () => number; rng: Rng; lookAheadSec?: number (default 1.2); phraseThreshold?: number (default 0.25) })`. `engine` is the layer-1 bundle {graph, alpha, x, eig} with step/inject — define a minimal `Engine` interface in this file; the metastable engine task will implement it too.
- `on('note'|'form'|'tick', cb)`, `start()`, `stop()`, `inject(node)` (game->music stinger), live-settable `tempo` and `alpha`.
- Internally: a `pump()` method that runs the look-ahead loop; the caller (or an internal setInterval at ~100ms, injectable for tests) invokes it. Per step reproduce beat() logic: melody NoteEvent with velocity 0.10 + 0.30*min(1, tension/1.4), pan spread by node index; ornament event with probability 0.5 when tension > 1.1; up to 3 pad events from modeCoeffs (amp threshold 0.05, strongest 2 same-sign eigenvector components — port mixing-time-composer.html lines 233-249); cadence NoteEvent one octave down + phraseResolved via CrossingDetector, then inject(randomNode).
- NO WebAudio, NO Tone.js, NO DOM in this module. Time is only ever read through the injected clock.

## Acceptance criteria (vitest, fake clock)
- With a manually advanced fake clock: all emitted event times are >= clock time at emission and monotonically ordered per layer.
- Advance clock by 10 simulated seconds: number of melody events ~= tempo * 10 (within 1).
- Phrase test: run until tension < 0.25 -> exactly one phraseResolved fires, a cadence event accompanies it, and x is re-concentrated (max(x) >= 0.7).
- Late-wake test: jump fake clock forward 5s -> no event burst; next event time is within lookAhead of the new now.
- Determinism: same seed + same fake clock script => identical event streams.
close_reason: Conductor done: events + look-ahead scheduler + beat() port. 6 fake-clock tests pass (ordering, count, phrase+cadence+reseed, late-wake resync, determinism). No WebAudio/DOM. Committed f65658f.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Done. src/conductor/{events,conductor}.ts. Engine interface + createEngine factory (composes core). Conductor: on/start/stop/inject/live tempo+alpha, pump() look-ahead loop (resync on nextTime<now-0.8, emit while nextTime<now+lookAhead, clamp emit time>=now), per-step beat() port (melody+ornament+top-3 pads+phrase-via-CrossingDetector->cadence+reseed). 6 fake-clock tests cover all 5 acceptance criteria + live setters. NOTE: phraseResolved uses CrossingDetector (downward crossing), so ultra-fast-mixing graphs (e.g. complete) whose tension is already <threshold at the first observed step never fire a phrase - this is correct crossing semantics; medium graphs (ring first-phrase@step13, smallworld@6, bipartite@2, clusters@212) fire normally. Determinism test uses ring. Boundary: no WebAudio/Tone/DOM; time only via injected clock. Committed f65658f.

title: Metastable engine: hierarchical timescales (phrase/section/movement)
description:
## Context
The metastable engine is v1's flagship: nested weakly-coupled clusters make ONE random walk produce note-, phrase-, section- and movement-level form simultaneously (the fix for Markov music's flat long-range structure). Source: `convergence-suite.html` mode 5 `mMeta` (lines 710-745) plus the helpers `makeHier`/`hierStep`/`injectHier`/`tv3`/`padModes` (lines 272-322). The graph/metrics parts are already in core (buildHierarchicalGraph, tvHierarchical) — this task wires them into an Engine the Conductor can drive.

## Deliverables — src/core/engines/metastable.ts (+ export a plain mixing-time engine)
- `createMixingTimeEngine({ topology, alpha, seed }): Engine` — wraps buildGraph + stepChain + inject + jacobi into the Engine interface defined by the conductor task.
- `createMetastableEngine({ clusters=4, perCluster=4, superGroups=2, leak: { cluster: 0.05, super: 0.006 }, alpha=0.3, seed }): Engine & { grouping }` — additionally exposes `tvHierarchical` readings per step.
- Conductor integration: when the engine exposes a grouping, the Conductor fires 'sectionResolved' (supergroup TV crosses 0.25) and 'movementResolved' (full TV crosses 0.25 -> re-inject), each via its own CrossingDetector with the source's cooldown of 5 steps (convergence-suite.html lines 724-733). Register mapping: each cluster owns one octave register — port `clPitch` idea (line 155-156): pitchMap node index = cluster*perCluster + position, so cluster index selects register. Document this in the PitchMap sizing.
- `rebuild(newLeaks)` method on the engine — re-run buildHierarchicalGraph + jacobi, KEEP current x (renormalized onto same node count). The mood-surface task needs this hook.

## Acceptance criteria (vitest)
- Simulated run (fake clock, seed 7): phraseResolved count > sectionResolved count > movementResolved count over 2000 steps, all >= 1.
- Every movementResolved is preceded by >= 1 sectionResolved since the last movementResolved (hierarchy ordering).
- rebuild() preserves sum(x) === 1 and does not fire spurious form events on the next step.
close_reason: Metastable engine + hierarchical conductor form events done faithfully to mMeta. Criteria #2/#3 pass; #1 (strict count ordering) provably unreachable with movement-only reinject - reconciled via .14. 5 tests, 56 total. Committed eebaa28.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Done. Relocated Engine+createEngine to src/core/engine.ts (conductor re-exports) to keep core/engines in-layer. src/core/engines/metastable.ts: createMixingTimeEngine + createMetastableEngine (grouping, hierarchicalTV(), rebuild()). Conductor detects engine.grouping and fires phrase(cluster TV)/section(superg TV)/movement(full TV->reseed) via 3 CrossingDetectors (section/movement cooldown 5); resets detectors on rebuild (watches eig ref) for criterion #3. 5 new tests, 56 total. Boundary clean. CAVEAT: criterion #1 (phrase>section>movement) unreachable - movement-only reinject (per deliverable) makes each scale cross once/epoch (measured 60/60/60) and data-processing forces section>=phrase>=movement (reverse of stated). Criteria #2 (section-before-movement) and #3 (rebuild) PASS. Filed .14 with per-scale-re-excitation fix option for human. Committed eebaa28.

title: Mood surface: axes, presets, glide vs rebuild-crossfade
description:
## Context
Layer 2 of the convergence library: the mood toggles a game touches. The math params are honestly meaningful — laziness alpha = patience/smoothness; hierarchical leak weights = how far the melody wanders; tension->velocity mapping = urgency. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: some params change continuously WITHOUT rebuilding the chain (alpha shifts eigenvalues affinely, eigenvectors unchanged — see lazyLambda; also tempo and velocity mappings), others REQUIRE rebuilding (topology, leak weights). Reference for a mood morph done live in a game: `tiny-planet-r185-duet.html` nightF/dayF usage (lines 1464-1468, 1483-1498, 1604-1609) — register shift + timbre swap driven by game state.

## Deliverables — src/mood/surface.ts, src/mood/presets.ts
- `type Mood = { patience: number; wander: number; urgency: number }` all 0..1.
- Mappings (document each as a table in JSDoc): patience -> alpha = 0.95*patience (GLIDE); wander -> leak.cluster log-interpolated 0.005..0.3 and leak.super 0.001..0.05 (REBUILD); urgency -> velocity gain 0.5..1.5 x, ornament probability 0..0.6, tempo multiplier 0.8..1.3 (GLIDE).
- `class MoodSurface { constructor(conductor); set(partial: Partial<Mood>, opts?: { glideSec }); preset(name): void; current(): Mood }`
- GLIDE params: exponential smoothing toward target applied once per conductor step (time constant default 2s).
- REBUILD params: call engine.rebuild(newLeaks) behind a crossfade: for N=8 steps, scale outgoing engine's event velocities by (1-k/N) while the rebuilt chain warms up, then re-inject at the node where max(x) currently sits (NOT random — preserves melodic continuity). Must not emit a discontinuity: assert no step emits zero events during the crossfade if both sides would have emitted.
- Presets in presets.ts with one-line musical intent comments: `calm` {patience .8, wander .15, urgency .1}, `explore` {.45, .5, .35}, `tense` {.15, .3, .85}, `night` {.7, .25, .2} + register/brightness hint passed through to renderer options.

## Acceptance criteria (vitest, fake clock)
- set({patience: 0}) -> alpha reaches < 0.05 within 3 time constants, monotonically.
- set({wander: 1}) triggers exactly one rebuild; event stream across the crossfade window has no gap > 2 step durations.
- preset('tense') then preset('calm') round-trips without error and current() reflects targets after settling.

title: WebAudio renderer: bundled instrument palette + ombak
description:
## Context
Layer 4 of the convergence library: batteries-included sound so demos work out of the box; games can skip it and consume events directly. Source: the shared palette in `convergence-suite.html` lines 182-258 — `bar` (additive partials with per-partial decay), `strike` (detuned bar pair), `strikeB` (hollow/woody partial set), `gong` (pitch-settling partials), `kick`, `hat`, `noiseBurst`, `bassHit`. Plus the gamelan ombak effect from `mixing-time-composer-gamelan.html`: every strike is a PAIR of voices detuned by a few Hz (pengumbang/pengisep beating — search "ombak" in that file).

## Deliverables — src/render/webaudio/
- `palette.ts`: port the 8 instrument functions, refactored to take `(ctx: AudioContext, dest: AudioNode, when: number, ...)` — CRITICAL: schedule at the EVENT time `when`, never at ctx.currentTime (the conductor emits future-dated events; using currentTime would destroy the look-ahead timing).
- `renderer.ts`: `class WebAudioRenderer { constructor(ctx, opts?: { gain=0.42, ombakHz?: number }); attach(conductor): () => void }` — builds master gain -> DynamicsCompressor -> destination (suite lines 186-188); subscribes to note events; layer mapping: melody->strike, ornament->strike at 0.7 velocity, pad->bar with sine partials, cadence->gong. If `ombakHz` is set, melody/pad voices double with +/- ombakHz/2 detune.
- `demos/renderer-smoke.html`: minimal page — one button, creates AudioContext + mixing-time engine + conductor + renderer, plays. Must run by opening the file with `npx vite` (add vite as devDependency, root-level `vite.config.ts` serving demos/ with the lib aliased to src/).

## Acceptance criteria
- `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` clean; palette module has no DOM dependencies (AudioContext passed in, so it can run under a mock in tests).
- Unit test with a stub AudioContext (record calls): a NoteEvent at time T schedules oscillator start(T) not start(currentTime); velocity maps to gain peak.
- Manual: renderer-smoke.html produces sound in Chrome; note in the issue comments that this was verified.
close_reason: WebAudio palette+renderer+ombak done. Automated acceptance passes (stub-ctx scheduling/velocity tests, typecheck, build, vite build of demo). Manual Chrome sound-check flagged as pending for a human (no browser in this env). Committed 2d21302.

comments.author: johnfewell
comments.text: Done. src/render/webaudio/{palette,renderer}.ts. Palette: 8 synths ported, refactored to (ctx,dest,when,...) scheduling at EVENT time (not currentTime); no DOM deps (grep-verified), noise buffer cached per ctx. Renderer: WebAudioRenderer(ctx,{gain,ombakHz}); attach returns unsubscribe; master gain->compressor->destination; layer map melody/pad->strike/bar, ornament->strike, cadence->gong; ombakHz doubles melody/pad into +/-ombakHz/2 pair. demos/renderer-smoke.html + vite.config.ts (demos root, lib aliased to src) + demo script; vitest.config.ts keeps test root at project root. 3 stub-ctx tests pass (start(T) not start(currentTime); velocity->gain peak; unsubscribe). typecheck+build clean. VERIFIED demo bundles via 'vite build' (20 modules resolve, alias graph OK) and dev server serves 200 + transforms TS. MANUAL BROWSER CHECK PENDING: I cannot open Chrome/produce audio in this environment - a human must confirm renderer-smoke.html makes sound via 'npm run demo'. Committed 2d21302.
